articles.headline: Suspected Militants Kill Migrant Worker in J&K's Shopian, Heightening Security Concerns
articles.explainer:
• A migrant worker from Bihar was found shot dead by suspected militants in Shopian District, Jammu and Kashmir, triggering widespread condemnation.
• The incident highlights the persistent challenge of targeted killings in the region, aimed at creating fear and disrupting normalcy.
• Security forces have launched a massive search operation to apprehend the perpetrators and enhance vigil in sensitive areas.
• The killing underscores the vulnerability of non-local laborers who migrate to J&K for livelihood opportunities, impacting economic activities.
• Government reiterates its commitment to ensuring the safety of all citizens and workers in the Union Territory, while condemning acts of terror.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: Shopian District is located in the Kashmir Valley.
Targeted killings in J&K often involve non-local workers or minority community members.
The incident occurred in the Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of militancy in J&K, focusing on targeted killings and their objectives.
Analyze the socio-economic impact of such incidents on migrant laborers and the regional economy.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current security strategies in protecting vulnerable populations in J&K.
Suggest comprehensive measures to address both the security challenges and the economic aspirations of the people in the region.
articles.imageDescription: Security personnel conducting a search operation in a rural area of Shopian, J&K.
articles.trivia: Shopian is historically known as the 'Apple Town' due to its extensive apple orchards.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Linkages of organized crime with terrorism; various security forces and agencies and their mandate.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Refuses Review of Electoral Bonds Verdict, Reiterates Transparency Mandate
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today dismissed a batch of review petitions challenging its February 2024 judgment that struck down the Electoral Bonds scheme as unconstitutional.
• The bench reiterated its stance on the importance of transparency in political funding for a healthy democracy.
• The original judgment had mandated the Election Commission of India (ECI) to publish details of electoral bonds purchased and redeemed.
• Critics of the scheme had argued it promoted opacity and anonymous donations, leading to a lack of accountability.
• This decision reinforces the judiciary's role in upholding democratic principles and ensuring fair electoral practices.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary—Ministries and Departments of the Government; pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
prelimsPoints: The Electoral Bonds scheme was introduced in 2017.
The Supreme Court declared the scheme unconstitutional in February 2024.
The Election Commission of India (ECI) was tasked with publishing donor and recipient data.
The current ruling is on review petitions against the earlier judgment.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's decision on political funding and electoral transparency in India.
Discuss the arguments for and against the Electoral Bonds scheme, highlighting its impact on democratic accountability.
Examine the role of the judiciary in safeguarding constitutional values and promoting electoral reforms.
Propose alternative mechanisms for transparent political funding that balance privacy and public interest.
articles.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building in New Delhi.
articles.trivia: The Supreme Court of India came into being on January 26, 1950, with the inauguration of the Constitution of India.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.area: Structure, Organization and Functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Judicial Review; Salient features of the Representation of People's Act.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflationary Pressures and Global Uncertainty
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time.
• The decision was driven by persistent inflationary pressures, particularly in food items, and global economic uncertainties.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das emphasized the MPC's commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth.
• The central bank also maintained its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance, signaling continued vigilance against inflation.
• Analysts suggest that future rate decisions will hinge on the trajectory of inflation, monsoon performance, and global commodity prices.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.

Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
prelimsPoints: The current repo rate is 6.5%.
The MPC consists of six members (3 from RBI, 3 external).
The primary objective of monetary policy is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
The 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance implies reducing liquidity in the system.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the status quo on the repo rate in the current economic scenario.
Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth.
Discuss the potential impact of sustained high interest rates on various sectors of the Indian economy.
Suggest measures the government can take, in conjunction with monetary policy, to address inflationary pressures and stimulate growth.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, in accordance with the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: RBI and its functions; Inflation and its control.

articles.headline: India Deepens Strategic Partnership with ASEAN on Trade, Connectivity, and Maritime Security
articles.explainer:
• India participated in the annual ASEAN-India Summit, reaffirming its commitment to strengthening ties with the Southeast Asian bloc.
• Key discussions focused on enhancing trade and investment, particularly through the review of the ASEAN-India Free Trade Area (AIFTA).
• Agreements were reached on improving physical and digital connectivity, including infrastructure projects and cross-border digital payment systems.
• Maritime security cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region was a significant agenda item, emphasizing freedom of navigation and adherence to international law.
• India's 'Act East Policy' continues to be the cornerstone of its engagement with ASEAN, fostering comprehensive strategic partnership.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: ASEAN was founded in 1967.
India became a Sectoral Dialogue Partner of ASEAN in 1992, a Dialogue Partner in 1996, and a Summit-level Partner in 2002.
The 'Act East Policy' was launched in 2014, succeeding the 'Look East Policy'.
The Indo-Pacific region is crucial for global trade and security.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India's strategic partnership with ASEAN in the context of its Act East Policy and Indo-Pacific vision.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in enhancing trade and economic cooperation between India and ASEAN.
Examine how increased connectivity projects can boost regional integration and India's influence in Southeast Asia.
Evaluate the role of India-ASEAN cooperation in addressing maritime security concerns and maintaining regional stability.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states displayed during a summit meeting.
articles.trivia: ASEAN comprises ten member states: Brunei, Cambodia, Indonesia, Laos, Malaysia, Myanmar, Philippines, Singapore, Thailand, and Vietnam.

prelimsSyllabus.area: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Key Abort Test for Gaganyaan Mission, Paving Way for Manned Flight
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today successfully conducted a critical in-flight abort test for its Gaganyaan human spaceflight mission.
• The test, 'Crew Escape System Demonstration-2 (CESD-2)', validated the safety mechanisms designed to rescue astronauts in case of an anomaly during launch.
• This achievement marks a significant milestone in India's ambitious Gaganyaan project, moving closer to sending Indian astronauts into space.
• The Crew Escape System is crucial for ensuring the safety of the crew module by quickly separating it from the launch vehicle during an emergency.
• ISRO Chairman highlighted the indigenous development of all key technologies, reinforcing India's self-reliance in space exploration.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight mission.
The mission aims to send Indian astronauts to an orbit of 400 km for a 3-day mission.
LVM3 (formerly GSLV Mk-III) is the designated launch vehicle for Gaganyaan.
Vyommitra is the humanoid robot designed to fly on uncrewed Gaganyaan missions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance and scientific objectives of India's Gaganyaan mission.
Explain the critical technologies involved in ensuring astronaut safety during human spaceflight, with a focus on the Crew Escape System.
Analyze the broader implications of India's indigenous human spaceflight capabilities for its global standing in space technology.
Examine the potential socio-economic benefits and scientific spin-offs from such ambitious space missions.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic representation of the Gaganyaan Crew Escape System separating from the launch vehicle during an abort test.
articles.trivia: If successful, India will become the fourth nation to independently launch humans into space, after the USSR (now Russia), USA, and China.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Science and Technology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Indian Space Programme; Space missions and their applications.

articles.headline: Government Sets Ambitious New Targets for Green Hydrogen Production Under National Mission
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government today announced enhanced targets for green hydrogen production, aiming to position India as a global hub for the clean fuel.
• The updated National Green Hydrogen Mission now seeks to achieve 10 million tonnes per annum (MTPA) production capacity by 2030, up from previous estimates.
• This move is aimed at accelerating decarbonization of key industrial sectors, reducing reliance on fossil fuel imports, and creating green job opportunities.
• Incentives for manufacturing electrolysers and producing green hydrogen are expected to drive private sector investment.
• India's abundant renewable energy resources provide a strong foundation for scaling up green hydrogen production.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: The National Green Hydrogen Mission was approved in January 2023.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
It aims to reduce India's import bill for fossil fuels.
The target year for significant production capacity is 2030.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate change mitigation goals.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production in India, considering technological and economic factors.
Evaluate the potential impact of the National Green Hydrogen Mission on various industrial sectors and job creation.
Suggest policy interventions and international collaborations needed to accelerate India's transition to a green hydrogen economy.
articles.imageDescription: A conceptual image showing a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the Universe, but rarely found in its pure elemental form on Earth.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Environment and Ecology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Renewable energy and climate change initiatives.

articles.headline: Delimitation Commission Formation for Women's Reservation Bill Under Active Consideration
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Law and Justice announced that the formation of a Delimitation Commission to implement the Women's Reservation Bill (Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam) is under active consideration.
• The Bill, passed in 2023, reserves one-third of seats for women in the Lok Sabha and State Assemblies, but its implementation is contingent on delimitation and census.
• The Delimitation Commission will redraw constituency boundaries based on the latest census figures, which are yet to be conducted.
• This process is crucial for identifying the specific seats to be reserved for women on a rotational basis.
• The government aims to expedite the necessary steps for the early implementation of this landmark legislation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: The Women's Reservation Bill was passed as the 106th Constitutional Amendment Act.
It reserves 33% of seats for women in Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies.
Implementation requires a new census and subsequent delimitation.
Delimitation is the act of redrawing boundaries of electoral constituencies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Women's Reservation Bill in promoting gender equality and political empowerment in India.
Explain the process and challenges involved in the delimitation exercise, particularly in the context of the Women's Reservation Bill.
Analyze the potential impact of rotational reservation on political stability and constituency development.
Suggest measures to expedite the census and delimitation process while ensuring fairness and transparency.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image of the Indian Parliament building with a focus on women's representation.
articles.trivia: The first attempt to introduce a women's reservation bill was in 1996, but it failed to pass.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.area: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population; Constitutional Amendments.

articles.headline: Election Commission Announces Dates for Key State Assembly Polls, Model Code of Conduct Enforced
articles.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) today announced the schedule for upcoming Assembly elections in three states: Jharkhand, Maharashtra, and Haryana.
• The Model Code of Conduct (MCC) has come into immediate effect in these states, restricting government announcements and activities that could influence voters.
• ECI emphasized its commitment to conducting free, fair, and transparent elections, ensuring a level playing field for all political parties.
• Extensive preparations include deployment of security forces, voter awareness campaigns, and technological interventions for smooth polling.
• The announcement sets the stage for intense political campaigning and scrutiny of electoral processes.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The Election Commission of India is an autonomous constitutional authority.
Article 324 of the Indian Constitution provides for the ECI.
The Model Code of Conduct (MCC) is a set of guidelines issued by the ECI for political parties and candidates.
Jharkhand, Maharashtra, and Haryana are the states where elections are announced.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and powers of the Election Commission of India in ensuring free and fair elections in India.
Explain the significance of the Model Code of Conduct (MCC) and its effectiveness in regulating political behavior during elections.
Analyze the challenges faced by the ECI in conducting elections in diverse and complex electoral landscapes.
Suggest electoral reforms that could further strengthen the integrity and transparency of the Indian electoral system.
articles.imageDescription: The logo of the Election Commission of India with an illustrative ballot box.
articles.trivia: The first general election in India was held from 1951 to 1952, taking four months to complete.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Election Commission; Salient features of the Representation of People's Act.

articles.headline: Union Cabinet Approves Major Economic Corridor Project to Boost Regional Connectivity and Trade
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet today approved a new multi-modal economic corridor project connecting key industrial hubs across three states.
• The project, estimated at ₹50,000 crores, aims to enhance logistics efficiency, reduce transportation costs, and facilitate faster movement of goods and people.
• It integrates various modes of transport, including highways, railways, and dedicated freight corridors, under the PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan.
• The corridor is expected to stimulate economic growth, attract investments, and create numerous employment opportunities in the region.
• This initiative is a crucial step towards improving India's infrastructure backbone and fostering regional development.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan was launched in October 2021.
It is a digital platform to bring 16 ministries together for integrated planning and coordinated implementation of infrastructure connectivity projects.
Economic corridors are vital for improving logistics performance index.
Investment models often include Public-Private Partnerships (PPP).
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of multi-modal economic corridors in accelerating India's economic growth and global competitiveness.
Discuss how the PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan integrates various infrastructure projects and ensures synergy.
Examine the potential socio-economic benefits and environmental considerations associated with large-scale infrastructure projects.
Suggest measures to overcome challenges in project implementation, such as land acquisition, funding, and inter-ministerial coordination.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of a modern highway interchange with rail lines in the background, symbolizing an economic corridor.
articles.trivia: India aims to reduce logistics costs as a percentage of GDP, which is currently higher than developed economies.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Investment models; PM Gati Shakti.

articles.headline: India and Nepal Hold Constructive Talks on Border Issues, Focus on Bilateral Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• High-level delegations from India and Nepal met today to discuss long-standing border issues and explore avenues for enhanced bilateral cooperation.
• The talks aimed at resolving the boundary dispute in areas like Kalapani, Lipulekh, and Limpiyadhura through diplomatic dialogue.
• Both sides emphasized the unique historical and cultural ties between the two nations and the importance of mutual trust and respect.
• Discussions also covered cooperation in energy, connectivity, trade, and cultural exchange, reaffirming the comprehensive nature of their relationship.
• This engagement signals a renewed commitment to addressing differences amicably and strengthening the 'Roti-Beti Ka Rishta'.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: Kalapani, Lipulekh, and Limpiyadhura are disputed territories between India and Nepal.
The Treaty of Sugauli (1816) defined the Kali River as the western boundary of Nepal.
India and Nepal share an open border.
Nepal is a landlocked country, heavily reliant on India for transit and trade.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and complexities of the India-Nepal border dispute, particularly concerning Kalapani, Lipulekh, and Limpiyadhura.
Discuss the strategic importance of Nepal for India's security and regional stability.
Examine the various dimensions of India-Nepal bilateral cooperation beyond border issues, such as trade, energy, and cultural ties.
Suggest diplomatic strategies and confidence-building measures to foster a stronger and more stable relationship between the two nations.
articles.imageDescription: A handshake between Indian and Nepalese officials, symbolizing bilateral talks.
articles.trivia: The 'Roti-Beti ka Rishta' (relationship of bread and daughter) refers to the deep socio-cultural ties and cross-border marriages between India and Nepal.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Bilateral relations with Nepal; Border disputes.

articles.headline: Government Launches Pilot Project to Integrate AI in Public Service Delivery
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) today launched a pilot project to integrate Artificial Intelligence (AI) into key public service delivery mechanisms.
• The initiative aims to enhance efficiency, transparency, and accessibility of government services for citizens across select domains.
• Initial applications include AI-powered chatbots for grievance redressal, predictive analytics for resource allocation, and intelligent systems for document verification.
• The project emphasizes ethical AI development, data privacy, and inclusive design to ensure equitable access and prevent bias.
• This move signifies India's commitment to leveraging emerging technologies for 'Good Governance' and 'Digital India' initiatives.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
prelimsPoints: MeitY is the nodal ministry for IT, electronics, and internet policy.
Digital India program was launched in 2015.
AI can be used for predictive analysis, chatbots, and automation.
Ethical AI focuses on fairness, accountability, and transparency.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential benefits and challenges of integrating Artificial Intelligence into public service delivery in India.
Analyze the ethical considerations and data privacy concerns associated with the use of AI in governance.
Examine how AI can contribute to achieving the objectives of 'Good Governance' and 'Digital India'.
Suggest a framework for the responsible and inclusive deployment of AI technologies in public administration.
articles.imageDescription: A visual representation of AI interacting with government data and citizens, symbolizing e-governance.
articles.trivia: India is among the top countries globally in terms of AI skill penetration and talent availability.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Science and Technology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Artificial Intelligence and its applications; E-governance.

articles.headline: Expert Panel Recommends Stricter Measures for Conservation in Ecologically Sensitive Western Ghats
articles.explainer:
• An expert committee constituted by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change submitted its report recommending stricter conservation norms for the Western Ghats.
• The report emphasizes the need to declare certain areas as Ecologically Sensitive Zones (ESZs) with regulated developmental activities.
• It highlights the immense biodiversity of the Western Ghats, which is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a global biodiversity hotspot.
• The recommendations aim to balance ecological preservation with the livelihood needs of local communities.
• Previous reports by Gadgil and Kasturirangan committees on Western Ghats conservation are also referenced, indicating ongoing policy debates.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Bio-diversity and Environment.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats span six states: Gujarat, Maharashtra, Goa, Karnataka, Kerala, and Tamil Nadu.
It is one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
The Gadgil Committee (Western Ghats Ecology Expert Panel - WGEEP) and Kasturirangan Committee (High-Level Working Group - HLWG) are key reports on the Western Ghats.
ESZs are areas notified under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats and the threats it faces from developmental activities.
Compare and contrast the recommendations of the Gadgil and Kasturirangan committees on Western Ghats conservation.
Analyze the challenges in implementing conservation policies in ecologically sensitive areas, particularly concerning local communities' livelihoods.
Suggest a balanced approach to ensure sustainable development while preserving the unique biodiversity of the Western Ghats.
articles.imageDescription: A lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with misty mountains and dense forest.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayan mountain range.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Environment and Ecology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Conservation of biodiversity; Ecologically Sensitive Zones.

articles.headline: Ministry of Education Releases Progress Report on National Education Policy 2020 Implementation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education today released a comprehensive progress report detailing the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report highlights achievements in curriculum reforms, integration of vocational education, and digital learning initiatives across various levels.
• Key focus areas include foundational literacy and numeracy, multidisciplinary education, and teacher training programs.
• Challenges related to infrastructure, faculty recruitment, and equitable access in remote areas were also acknowledged.
• The government reaffirms its commitment to transforming India's education landscape to meet 21st-century demands.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry —the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
The policy aims for universalization of education from pre-school to secondary level.
It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
Digital learning initiatives include DIKSHA platform and SWAYAM.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key reforms proposed by the National Education Policy 2020 and their potential to transform India's education system.
Discuss the progress made in implementing NEP 2020, highlighting both successes and persistent challenges.
Examine the role of digital education and vocational training in achieving the goals of NEP 2020 and addressing the skill gap.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access to quality education and overcome infrastructural and faculty-related hurdles in NEP implementation.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting engaged in learning, symbolizing the goals of NEP 2020.
articles.trivia: NEP 2020 recommends increasing public investment in education to 6% of GDP.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Education.

articles.headline: India Strengthens Cyber Security Framework to Combat Rising Digital Threats
articles.explainer:
• The National Cyber Security Coordinator announced new measures to bolster India's cyber security framework amidst a surge in sophisticated digital threats.
• The updated strategy focuses on enhancing critical information infrastructure protection, improving incident response mechanisms, and fostering public-private partnerships.
• CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) has issued fresh advisories, urging organizations to strengthen their cyber resilience against ransomware and data breaches.
• Emphasis is also placed on developing indigenous cyber security products and services, and skill development in the sector.
• This proactive approach aims to safeguard national security, economic stability, and citizen data in the digital realm.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: CERT-In is the national agency for responding to computer security incidents.
Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) includes sectors like energy, transport, banking, and defense.
The National Cyber Security Policy was launched in 2013.
The Personal Data Protection Bill (now Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023) addresses data privacy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cyber threats in India and their implications for national security and economic stability.
Analyze the key components of India's cyber security framework and the role of various agencies like CERT-In.
Examine the challenges in protecting critical information infrastructure and citizen data in an increasingly digital world.
Suggest comprehensive strategies, including technological, legal, and international cooperation, to enhance India's cyber resilience.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image of a digital lock and shield, representing cyber security protection.
articles.trivia: India ranks among the top countries most affected by cyberattacks globally.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.

articles.headline: Government Reviews Agri-Export Policy to Boost Farmers' Income and Global Market Access
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry, in collaboration with the Ministry of Agriculture, initiated a review of India's Agri-Export Policy.
• The objective is to formulate strategies that boost agricultural exports, ensuring better price realization for farmers and higher income.
• Discussions focused on diversifying export baskets, addressing supply chain bottlenecks, and promoting value-added agricultural products.
• Measures include improving infrastructure for post-harvest management, cold storage facilities, and adherence to international quality standards.
• This move aligns with the government's vision of doubling farmers' income and enhancing India's presence in global agricultural markets.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security.
prelimsPoints: India's Agri-Export Policy was launched in 2018.
APEDA is responsible for the promotion of agricultural product exports.
The goal of doubling farmers' income by 2022 was set.
Supply chain management is crucial for reducing post-harvest losses.
mainsPoints: Analyze the current state of India's agricultural exports and the challenges faced by farmers in accessing global markets.
Discuss the various policy interventions required to boost agri-exports and achieve the objective of doubling farmers' income.
Examine the role of infrastructure development, food processing, and quality standards in enhancing the competitiveness of Indian agricultural products.
Suggest strategies for diversifying India's agri-export basket and integrating farmers into global value chains.
articles.imageDescription: Fresh fruits and vegetables being packed for export, symbolizing agricultural trade.
articles.trivia: India is one of the world's largest producers of several agricultural commodities, including milk, pulses, and spices.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; Food processing and related industries.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Temple Remains, Sparks Heritage Conservation Debate
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of significant ancient temple remains during an excavation in Uttar Pradesh.
• The unearthed structures and artifacts provide new insights into regional architectural styles and religious practices from centuries ago.
• The discovery has reignited debates on the challenges of heritage conservation, balancing development with archaeological preservation.
• ASI plans further exploration and scientific dating to ascertain the exact period and cultural context of the findings.
• Experts emphasize the need for robust policies and community involvement in protecting India's rich archaeological heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: ASI is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage in India.
Uttar Pradesh has numerous ancient sites and historical monuments.
Carbon dating is a common method for determining the age of organic materials.
The Ancient Monuments and Archaeological Sites and Remains Act, 1958, governs heritage protection.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing India's ancient history and cultural evolution.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India in protecting and preserving India's vast and diverse cultural heritage.
Examine the role of public awareness and community participation in effective heritage conservation.
Suggest policy measures to balance infrastructure development with the preservation of archaeological sites and ancient monuments.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully excavating ancient ruins at a site in Uttar Pradesh.
articles.trivia: The ASI was founded in 1861 by Alexander Cunningham, who also became its first Director-General.

articles.headline: Government Undertakes Comprehensive Review of National Logistics Policy to Enhance Global Competitiveness
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry initiated a comprehensive review of the National Logistics Policy (NLP) launched in 2022.
• The review aims to identify bottlenecks and suggest measures to further reduce logistics costs and improve efficiency across supply chains.
• Focus areas include digitalization of logistics processes, development of multi-modal logistics parks, and skill development in the sector.
• The policy seeks to integrate various modes of transport and leverage technology to create a seamless logistics ecosystem.
• Enhanced logistics efficiency is crucial for boosting India's manufacturing competitiveness and achieving a $5 trillion economy goal.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: The National Logistics Policy was launched in September 2022.
It aims to reduce logistics costs from 13-14% of GDP to 8% by 2030.
PM Gati Shakti is integral to the NLP for infrastructure development.
Multi-modal logistics parks (MMLPs) are a key component.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives and key pillars of India's National Logistics Policy and its significance for economic development.
Discuss the challenges faced by India's logistics sector and how the NLP aims to address them.
Examine the role of technology and multi-modal infrastructure in improving logistics efficiency and reducing costs.
Suggest further reforms and investments needed to make India's logistics sector globally competitive and support the 'Make in India' initiative.
articles.imageDescription: A large cargo ship being unloaded at a port, with trucks and trains in the background, representing integrated logistics.
articles.trivia: India's logistics sector is one of the largest employers, providing livelihood to over 22 million people.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Logistics sector; Government policies.

articles.headline: ICAR Develops New Climate-Resilient Crop Varieties to Combat Extreme Weather Events
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) announced the development of several new climate-resilient crop varieties.
• These varieties are engineered to withstand extreme weather conditions, including droughts, floods, and heatwaves, ensuring food security.
• The research focuses on enhancing crop productivity, nutritional value, and resistance to pests and diseases under changing climatic patterns.
• Farmers will receive training and support for adopting these new varieties, along with sustainable farming practices.
• This initiative is crucial for adapting Indian agriculture to climate change impacts and safeguarding farmers' livelihoods.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: ICAR is an autonomous organization under the Department of Agricultural Research and Education (DARE), Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare.
India is highly vulnerable to climate change impacts on agriculture.
Climate-resilient crops can tolerate abiotic stresses like drought and salinity.
Food security is a major concern for India's large population.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges posed by climate change to Indian agriculture and its implications for food security and farmers' livelihoods.
Explain the role of agricultural research institutions like ICAR in developing climate-resilient crop varieties and sustainable farming practices.
Analyze the importance of integrating traditional knowledge with modern biotechnology for climate adaptation in agriculture.
Suggest policy measures and extension services required to facilitate the adoption of new crop varieties by farmers and build climate resilience.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer inspecting healthy crops in a field, symbolizing agricultural resilience.
articles.trivia: ICAR, established in 1929, is one of the largest national agricultural systems in the world.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Agriculture
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Climate change impacts on agriculture; Agricultural research.

articles.headline: India's Second Indigenous Aircraft Carrier Commences Sea Trials, Boosting Naval Prowess
articles.explainer:
• India's second indigenously built aircraft carrier, INS Vikrant, today commenced its final phase of sea trials, moving closer to full operational deployment.
• The carrier, built by Cochin Shipyard Limited, showcases India's growing capabilities in naval shipbuilding and self-reliance in defense production.
• Its operationalization will significantly enhance the Indian Navy's blue-water capabilities and projection of power in the Indian Ocean Region.
• The trials involve rigorous testing of the propulsion system, aviation complex, and various onboard systems.
• This milestone underscores India's commitment to becoming a net security provider and strengthening its maritime domain awareness.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: INS Vikrant is India's first indigenously designed and built aircraft carrier.
It was built at Cochin Shipyard Limited.
India also operates INS Vikramaditya (modified Russian carrier).
Aircraft carriers are crucial for power projection and sea control.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of aircraft carriers for India's naval doctrine and its role in the Indian Ocean Region.
Analyze India's progress in indigenous defense production, with a specific focus on naval shipbuilding capabilities.
Examine the challenges and opportunities associated with maintaining and operating a carrier battle group.
Evaluate how the operationalization of INS Vikrant contributes to India's aspirations of becoming a net security provider in the Indo-Pacific.
articles.imageDescription: The majestic INS Vikrant at sea during its trials, with fighter jets on its deck.
articles.trivia: The original INS Vikrant, India's first aircraft carrier, was acquired from the UK in 1961 and played a crucial role in the 1971 Indo-Pak war.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.area: Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Indian Navy; Defence indigenisation.

articles.headline: PM Vishwakarma Scheme Shows Promising Progress in Empowering Artisans and Craftspeople
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) released a progress report on the PM Vishwakarma Scheme, highlighting its positive impact.
• The scheme, launched in 2023, aims to support traditional artisans and craftspeople ('Vishwakarmas') with skill training, toolkits, and credit linkage.
• Thousands of artisans across various trades have registered, receiving training and financial assistance to upgrade their skills and businesses.
• The initiative promotes the preservation of traditional crafts, fosters entrepreneurship, and integrates artisans into the formal economy.
• This scheme is a significant step towards empowering the informal sector and realizing the vision of 'Vocal for Local'.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Development processes and the development industry —the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: The PM Vishwakarma Kaushal Samman Yojana (PM-VIKAS) was launched on Vishwakarma Jayanti in September 2023.
It is a central sector scheme.
It provides support to artisans and craftspeople of 18 traditional trades.
Benefits include skill upgradation, toolkit incentive, and collateral-free credit.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and key features of the PM Vishwakarma Scheme in empowering traditional artisans and craftspeople in India.
Analyze the challenges faced by the informal sector, particularly artisans, and how such schemes address them.
Examine the socio-economic impact of promoting traditional crafts and integrating artisans into the formal economy.
Suggest measures to further strengthen the PM Vishwakarma Scheme and ensure its long-term sustainability and reach.
articles.imageDescription: An artisan meticulously working on a traditional craft, symbolizing skill and heritage.
articles.trivia: The scheme covers 18 traditional trades, including carpenters, blacksmiths, potters, sculptors, cobblers, and barbers.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Government schemes for skill development and entrepreneurship.

highlights: Suspected militants kill a migrant worker in J&K's Shopian, raising internal security concerns.
Supreme Court dismisses review petitions on Electoral Bonds, reinforcing transparency in political funding.
RBI maintains repo rate at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time amidst inflation and global uncertainty.
ISRO successfully conducts a crucial abort test for the Gaganyaan mission, a major step towards manned spaceflight.
Government sets ambitious new targets for green hydrogen production, aiming for 10 MTPA capacity by 2030.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to achieve 10 million tonnes per annum (MTPA) production capacity by 2030.
2. Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources.
3. The mission's primary objective is to reduce India's reliance on fossil fuel imports.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The National Green Hydrogen Mission, approved in January 2023, aims to achieve 10 MTPA green hydrogen production capacity by 2030. Green hydrogen is indeed produced by electrolyzing water using renewable energy. A key objective of the mission is to decarbonize industries and reduce India's dependence on fossil fuel imports, thereby enhancing energy security.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for conducting elections to the State Legislative Assemblies in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Election Commission of India (ECI) is an autonomous constitutional authority responsible for administering elections to the Lok Sabha, Rajya Sabha, State Legislative Assemblies, and the offices of the President and Vice President in India. State Election Commissions are responsible for conducting elections to Panchayats and Municipalities.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Western Ghats:
1. They are one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
2. The Western Ghats span across six states in India.
3. The Gadgil Committee and Kasturirangan Committee reports are associated with their conservation.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The Western Ghats are indeed a global biodiversity hotspot. They stretch across six Indian states: Gujarat, Maharashtra, Goa, Karnataka, Kerala, and Tamil Nadu. Both the Madhav Gadgil (Western Ghats Ecology Expert Panel - WGEEP) and K. Kasturirangan (High-Level Working Group - HLWG) committees were formed to recommend conservation measures for the Western Ghats.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Gaganyaan mission, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. It is India's first mission to send a humanoid robot into space.
2. The Crew Escape System is designed to rescue astronauts during an anomaly in the launch phase.
3. LVM3 is the designated launch vehicle for the mission.

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight mission, aiming to send humans into space. Vyommitra is the humanoid robot designed for uncrewed Gaganyaan missions, but the mission itself is not solely for the robot. Statement 2 is correct; the Crew Escape System is a critical safety mechanism. Statement 3 is correct; LVM3 (Launch Vehicle Mark-3, formerly GSLV Mk-III) is the designated launch vehicle for Gaganyaan.

prelimsMocks.question: The PM Vishwakarma Scheme, recently in news, is aimed at:
options: (a) Providing housing to urban poor through skill development.
(b) Empowering traditional artisans and craftspeople with skill training and credit support.
(c) Promoting digital literacy among rural women.
(d) Enhancing agricultural productivity through modern farming techniques.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Empowering traditional artisans and craftspeople with skill training and credit support.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The PM Vishwakarma Kaushal Samman Yojana (PM-VIKAS) or PM Vishwakarma Scheme, launched in 2023, is a central sector scheme aimed at providing end-to-end support to traditional artisans and craftspeople (Vishwakarmas) through skill upgradation, toolkit incentives, collateral-free credit, and marketing support.

mainsMocks.question: The killing of a migrant worker in J&K's Shopian highlights the persistent security challenges and socio-economic vulnerabilities in the region. Discuss the evolving nature of militancy in J&K and suggest comprehensive measures to ensure the safety of vulnerable populations while fostering economic development.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism; GS2: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the incident and its context. Discuss the shift from large-scale attacks to targeted killings, aiming to create fear and disrupt normalcy. Analyze socio-economic impacts on migrant workers and the regional economy. Suggest multi-pronged measures: enhanced intelligence, community policing, economic rehabilitation, political dialogue, and addressing radicalization.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's decision to dismiss review petitions on Electoral Bonds underscores the judiciary's commitment to transparency in political funding. Critically examine the implications of this verdict on electoral reforms and democratic accountability in India. What alternative mechanisms can be explored for transparent political financing?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure; Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act; Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin with the SC's verdict and its significance. Discuss the arguments for and against Electoral Bonds, focusing on transparency vs. donor privacy. Analyze the impact on political parties, corporate funding, and democratic accountability. Propose alternatives like National Electoral Fund, state funding of elections, and stricter disclosure norms for political parties and donors.

mainsMocks.question: India's ambitious National Green Hydrogen Mission and ISRO's Gaganyaan success signify a strong push towards self-reliance in cutting-edge technologies. Discuss how these initiatives contribute to India's energy security, climate goals, and global standing in science and technology. What challenges must be overcome for their successful long-term implementation?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life; Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Infrastructure: Energy; Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce both the Green Hydrogen Mission and Gaganyaan. For Green Hydrogen, discuss its role in energy security, decarbonization, and job creation. For Gaganyaan, highlight its impact on space capabilities, national pride, and technological spin-offs. Address common challenges: funding, technological hurdles, skilled manpower, policy consistency, and international cooperation for both initiatives.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Temple Remains, Sparks Heritage Conservation Debate
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of significant ancient temple remains during an excavation in Uttar Pradesh.
• The unearthed structures and artifacts provide new insights into regional architectural styles and religious practices from centuries ago.
• The discovery has reignited debates on the challenges of heritage conservation, balancing development with archaeological preservation.
• ASI plans further exploration and scientific dating to ascertain the exact period and cultural context of the findings.
• Experts emphasize the need for robust policies and community involvement in protecting India's rich archaeological heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: ASI is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage in India.
Uttar Pradesh has numerous ancient sites and historical monuments.
Carbon dating is a common method for determining the age of organic materials.
The Ancient Monuments and Archaeological Sites and Remains Act, 1958, governs heritage protection.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing India's ancient history and cultural evolution.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India in protecting and preserving India's vast and diverse cultural heritage.
Examine the role of public awareness and community participation in effective heritage conservation.
Suggest policy measures to balance infrastructure development with the preservation of archaeological sites and ancient monuments.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully excavating ancient ruins at a site in Uttar Pradesh.
GS1.trivia: The ASI was founded in 1861 by Alexander Cunningham, who also became its first Director-General.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian History and Art & Culture
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Ancient Indian architecture; Role of ASI.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Refuses Review of Electoral Bonds Verdict, Reiterates Transparency Mandate
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today dismissed a batch of review petitions challenging its February 2024 judgment that struck down the Electoral Bonds scheme as unconstitutional.
• The bench reiterated its stance on the importance of transparency in political funding for a healthy democracy.
• The original judgment had mandated the Election Commission of India (ECI) to publish details of electoral bonds purchased and redeemed.
• Critics of the scheme had argued it promoted opacity and anonymous donations, leading to a lack of accountability.
• This decision reinforces the judiciary's role in upholding democratic principles and ensuring fair electoral practices.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary—Ministries and Departments of the Government; pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
prelimsPoints: The Electoral Bonds scheme was introduced in 2017.
The Supreme Court declared the scheme unconstitutional in February 2024.
The Election Commission of India (ECI) was tasked with publishing donor and recipient data.
The current ruling is on review petitions against the earlier judgment.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's decision on political funding and electoral transparency in India.
Discuss the arguments for and against the Electoral Bonds scheme, highlighting its impact on democratic accountability.
Examine the role of the judiciary in safeguarding constitutional values and promoting electoral reforms.
Propose alternative mechanisms for transparent political funding that balance privacy and public interest.
GS2.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building in New Delhi.
GS2.trivia: The Supreme Court of India came into being on January 26, 1950, with the inauguration of the Constitution of India.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.area: Structure, Organization and Functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Judicial Review; Salient features of the Representation of People's Act.

GS2.headline: India Deepens Strategic Partnership with ASEAN on Trade, Connectivity, and Maritime Security
GS2.explainer:
• India participated in the annual ASEAN-India Summit, reaffirming its commitment to strengthening ties with the Southeast Asian bloc.
• Key discussions focused on enhancing trade and investment, particularly through the review of the ASEAN-India Free Trade Area (AIFTA).
• Agreements were reached on improving physical and digital connectivity, including infrastructure projects and cross-border digital payment systems.
• Maritime security cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region was a significant agenda item, emphasizing freedom of navigation and adherence to international law.
• India's 'Act East Policy' continues to be the cornerstone of its engagement with ASEAN, fostering comprehensive strategic partnership.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: ASEAN was founded in 1967.
India became a Sectoral Dialogue Partner of ASEAN in 1992, a Dialogue Partner in 1996, and a Summit-level Partner in 2002.
The 'Act East Policy' was launched in 2014, succeeding the 'Look East Policy'.
The Indo-Pacific region is crucial for global trade and security.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India's strategic partnership with ASEAN in the context of its Act East Policy and Indo-Pacific vision.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in enhancing trade and economic cooperation between India and ASEAN.
Examine how increased connectivity projects can boost regional integration and India's influence in Southeast Asia.
Evaluate the role of India-ASEAN cooperation in addressing maritime security concerns and maintaining regional stability.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states displayed during a summit meeting.
GS2.trivia: ASEAN comprises ten member states: Brunei, Cambodia, Indonesia, Laos, Malaysia, Myanmar, Philippines, Singapore, Thailand, and Vietnam.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.area: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements
prelimsSyllabus.topic: India and its neighborhood; Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.

GS2.headline: Delimitation Commission Formation for Women's Reservation Bill Under Active Consideration
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Law and Justice announced that the formation of a Delimitation Commission to implement the Women's Reservation Bill (Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam) is under active consideration.
• The Bill, passed in 2023, reserves one-third of seats for women in the Lok Sabha and State Assemblies, but its implementation is contingent on delimitation and census.
• The Delimitation Commission will redraw constituency boundaries based on the latest census figures, which are yet to be conducted.
• This process is crucial for identifying the specific seats to be reserved for women on a rotational basis.
• The government aims to expedite the necessary steps for the early implementation of this landmark legislation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: The Women's Reservation Bill was passed as the 106th Constitutional Amendment Act.
It reserves 33% of seats for women in Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies.
Implementation requires a new census and subsequent delimitation.
Delimitation is the act of redrawing boundaries of electoral constituencies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Women's Reservation Bill in promoting gender equality and political empowerment in India.
Explain the process and challenges involved in the delimitation exercise, particularly in the context of the Women's Reservation Bill.
Analyze the potential impact of rotational reservation on political stability and constituency development.
Suggest measures to expedite the census and delimitation process while ensuring fairness and transparency.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized image of the Indian Parliament building with a focus on women's representation.
GS2.trivia: The first attempt to introduce a women's reservation bill was in 1996, but it failed to pass.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.area: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population; Constitutional Amendments.

GS2.headline: Election Commission Announces Dates for Key State Assembly Polls, Model Code of Conduct Enforced
GS2.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) today announced the schedule for upcoming Assembly elections in three states: Jharkhand, Maharashtra, and Haryana.
• The Model Code of Conduct (MCC) has come into immediate effect in these states, restricting government announcements and activities that could influence voters.
• ECI emphasized its commitment to conducting free, fair, and transparent elections, ensuring a level playing field for all political parties.
• Extensive preparations include deployment of security forces, voter awareness campaigns, and technological interventions for smooth polling.
• The announcement sets the stage for intense political campaigning and scrutiny of electoral processes.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: The Election Commission of India is an autonomous constitutional authority.
Article 324 of the Indian Constitution provides for the ECI.
The Model Code of Conduct (MCC) is a set of guidelines issued by the ECI for political parties and candidates.
Jharkhand, Maharashtra, and Haryana are the states where elections are announced.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and powers of the Election Commission of India in ensuring free and fair elections in India.
Explain the significance of the Model Code of Conduct (MCC) and its effectiveness in regulating political behavior during elections.
Analyze the challenges faced by the ECI in conducting elections in diverse and complex electoral landscapes.
Suggest electoral reforms that could further strengthen the integrity and transparency of the Indian electoral system.
GS2.imageDescription: The logo of the Election Commission of India with an illustrative ballot box.
GS2.trivia: The first general election in India was held from 1951 to 1952, taking four months to complete.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Election Commission; Salient features of the Representation of People's Act.

GS2.headline: India and Nepal Hold Constructive Talks on Border Issues, Focus on Bilateral Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• High-level delegations from India and Nepal met today to discuss long-standing border issues and explore avenues for enhanced bilateral cooperation.
• The talks aimed at resolving the boundary dispute in areas like Kalapani, Lipulekh, and Limpiyadhura through diplomatic dialogue.
• Both sides emphasized the unique historical and cultural ties between the two nations and the importance of mutual trust and respect.
• Discussions also covered cooperation in energy, connectivity, trade, and cultural exchange, reaffirming the comprehensive nature of their relationship.
• This engagement signals a renewed commitment to addressing differences amicably and strengthening the 'Roti-Beti Ka Rishta'.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: Kalapani, Lipulekh, and Limpiyadhura are disputed territories between India and Nepal.
The Treaty of Sugauli (1816) defined the Kali River as the western boundary of Nepal.
India and Nepal share an open border.
Nepal is a landlocked country, heavily reliant on India for transit and trade.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and complexities of the India-Nepal border dispute, particularly concerning Kalapani, Lipulekh, and Limpiyadhura.
Discuss the strategic importance of Nepal for India's security and regional stability.
Examine the various dimensions of India-Nepal bilateral cooperation beyond border issues, such as trade, energy, and cultural ties.
Suggest diplomatic strategies and confidence-building measures to foster a stronger and more stable relationship between the two nations.
GS2.imageDescription: A handshake between Indian and Nepalese officials, symbolizing bilateral talks.
GS2.trivia: The 'Roti-Beti ka Rishta' (relationship of bread and daughter) refers to the deep socio-cultural ties and cross-border marriages between India and Nepal.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Bilateral relations with Nepal; Border disputes.

GS2.headline: Ministry of Education Releases Progress Report on National Education Policy 2020 Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education today released a comprehensive progress report detailing the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report highlights achievements in curriculum reforms, integration of vocational education, and digital learning initiatives across various levels.
• Key focus areas include foundational literacy and numeracy, multidisciplinary education, and teacher training programs.
• Challenges related to infrastructure, faculty recruitment, and equitable access in remote areas were also acknowledged.
• The government reaffirms its commitment to transforming India's education landscape to meet 21st-century demands.

Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
The policy aims for universalization of education from pre-school to secondary level.
It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
Digital learning initiatives include DIKSHA platform and SWAYAM.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key reforms proposed by the National Education Policy 2020 and their potential to transform India's education system.
Discuss the progress made in implementing NEP 2020, highlighting both successes and persistent challenges.
Examine the role of digital education and vocational training in achieving the goals of NEP 2020 and addressing the skill gap.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access to quality education and overcome infrastructural and faculty-related hurdles in NEP implementation.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting engaged in learning, symbolizing the goals of NEP 2020.
GS2.trivia: NEP 2020 recommends increasing public investment in education to 6% of GDP.
