GS3.headline: Suspected Militants Kill Migrant Worker in J&K's Shopian, Heightening Security Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• A migrant worker from Bihar was found shot dead by suspected militants in Shopian District, Jammu and Kashmir, triggering widespread condemnation.
• The incident highlights the persistent challenge of targeted killings in the region, aimed at creating fear and disrupting normalcy.
• Security forces have launched a massive search operation to apprehend the perpetrators and enhance vigil in sensitive areas.
• The killing underscores the vulnerability of non-local laborers who migrate to J&K for livelihood opportunities, impacting economic activities.
• Government reiterates its commitment to ensuring the safety of all citizens and workers in the Union Territory, while condemning acts of terror.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: Shopian District is located in the Kashmir Valley.
Targeted killings in J&K often involve non-local workers or minority community members.
The incident occurred in the Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of militancy in J&K, focusing on targeted killings and their objectives.
Analyze the socio-economic impact of such incidents on migrant laborers and the regional economy.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current security strategies in protecting vulnerable populations in J&K.
Suggest comprehensive measures to address both the security challenges and the economic aspirations of the people in the region.
GS3.imageDescription: Security personnel conducting a search operation in a rural area of Shopian, J&K.
GS3.trivia: Shopian is historically known as the 'Apple Town' due to its extensive apple orchards.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Linkages of organized crime with terrorism; various security forces and agencies and their mandate.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflationary Pressures and Global Uncertainty
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time.
• The decision was driven by persistent inflationary pressures, particularly in food items, and global economic uncertainties.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das emphasized the MPC's commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth.
• The central bank also maintained its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance, signaling continued vigilance against inflation.
• Analysts suggest that future rate decisions will hinge on the trajectory of inflation, monsoon performance, and global commodity prices.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.

Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
prelimsPoints: The current repo rate is 6.5%.
The MPC consists of six members (3 from RBI, 3 external).
The primary objective of monetary policy is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
The 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance implies reducing liquidity in the system.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the status quo on the repo rate in the current economic scenario.
Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth.
Discuss the potential impact of sustained high interest rates on various sectors of the Indian economy.
Suggest measures the government can take, in conjunction with monetary policy, to address inflationary pressures and stimulate growth.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai.
GS3.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, in accordance with the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: RBI and its functions; Inflation and its control.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Key Abort Test for Gaganyaan Mission, Paving Way for Manned Flight
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today successfully conducted a critical in-flight abort test for its Gaganyaan human spaceflight mission.
• The test, 'Crew Escape System Demonstration-2 (CESD-2)', validated the safety mechanisms designed to rescue astronauts in case of an anomaly during launch.
• This achievement marks a significant milestone in India's ambitious Gaganyaan project, moving closer to sending Indian astronauts into space.
• The Crew Escape System is crucial for ensuring the safety of the crew module by quickly separating it from the launch vehicle during an emergency.
• ISRO Chairman highlighted the indigenous development of all key technologies, reinforcing India's self-reliance in space exploration.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight mission.
The mission aims to send Indian astronauts to an orbit of 400 km for a 3-day mission.
LVM3 (formerly GSLV Mk-III) is the designated launch vehicle for Gaganyaan.
Vyommitra is the humanoid robot designed to fly on uncrewed Gaganyaan missions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance and scientific objectives of India's Gaganyaan mission.
Explain the critical technologies involved in ensuring astronaut safety during human spaceflight, with a focus on the Crew Escape System.
Analyze the broader implications of India's indigenous human spaceflight capabilities for its global standing in space technology.
Examine the potential socio-economic benefits and scientific spin-offs from such ambitious space missions.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic representation of the Gaganyaan Crew Escape System separating from the launch vehicle during an abort test.
GS3.trivia: If successful, India will become the fourth nation to independently launch humans into space, after the USSR (now Russia), USA, and China.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Science and Technology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Indian Space Programme; Space missions and their applications.

GS3.headline: Government Sets Ambitious New Targets for Green Hydrogen Production Under National Mission
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government today announced enhanced targets for green hydrogen production, aiming to position India as a global hub for the clean fuel.
• The updated National Green Hydrogen Mission now seeks to achieve 10 million tonnes per annum (MTPA) production capacity by 2030, up from previous estimates.
• This move is aimed at accelerating decarbonization of key industrial sectors, reducing reliance on fossil fuel imports, and creating green job opportunities.
• Incentives for manufacturing electrolysers and producing green hydrogen are expected to drive private sector investment.
• India's abundant renewable energy resources provide a strong foundation for scaling up green hydrogen production.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: The National Green Hydrogen Mission was approved in January 2023.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
It aims to reduce India's import bill for fossil fuels.
The target year for significant production capacity is 2030.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate change mitigation goals.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production in India, considering technological and economic factors.
Evaluate the potential impact of the National Green Hydrogen Mission on various industrial sectors and job creation.
Suggest policy interventions and international collaborations needed to accelerate India's transition to a green hydrogen economy.
GS3.imageDescription: A conceptual image showing a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the Universe, but rarely found in its pure elemental form on Earth.

prelimsSyllabus.topic: Renewable energy and climate change initiatives.

GS3.headline: Union Cabinet Approves Major Economic Corridor Project to Boost Regional Connectivity and Trade
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet today approved a new multi-modal economic corridor project connecting key industrial hubs across three states.
• The project, estimated at ₹50,000 crores, aims to enhance logistics efficiency, reduce transportation costs, and facilitate faster movement of goods and people.
• It integrates various modes of transport, including highways, railways, and dedicated freight corridors, under the PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan.
• The corridor is expected to stimulate economic growth, attract investments, and create numerous employment opportunities in the region.
• This initiative is a crucial step towards improving India's infrastructure backbone and fostering regional development.

Investment models.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan was launched in October 2021.
It is a digital platform to bring 16 ministries together for integrated planning and coordinated implementation of infrastructure connectivity projects.
Economic corridors are vital for improving logistics performance index.
Investment models often include Public-Private Partnerships (PPP).
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of multi-modal economic corridors in accelerating India's economic growth and global competitiveness.
Discuss how the PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan integrates various infrastructure projects and ensures synergy.
Examine the potential socio-economic benefits and environmental considerations associated with large-scale infrastructure projects.
Suggest measures to overcome challenges in project implementation, such as land acquisition, funding, and inter-ministerial coordination.
GS3.imageDescription: An aerial view of a modern highway interchange with rail lines in the background, symbolizing an economic corridor.
GS3.trivia: India aims to reduce logistics costs as a percentage of GDP, which is currently higher than developed economies.

GS3.headline: Government Launches Pilot Project to Integrate AI in Public Service Delivery
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) today launched a pilot project to integrate Artificial Intelligence (AI) into key public service delivery mechanisms.
• The initiative aims to enhance efficiency, transparency, and accessibility of government services for citizens across select domains.
• Initial applications include AI-powered chatbots for grievance redressal, predictive analytics for resource allocation, and intelligent systems for document verification.
• The project emphasizes ethical AI development, data privacy, and inclusive design to ensure equitable access and prevent bias.
• This move signifies India's commitment to leveraging emerging technologies for 'Good Governance' and 'Digital India' initiatives.

Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
prelimsPoints: MeitY is the nodal ministry for IT, electronics, and internet policy.
Digital India program was launched in 2015.
AI can be used for predictive analysis, chatbots, and automation.
Ethical AI focuses on fairness, accountability, and transparency.

Examine how AI can contribute to achieving the objectives of 'Good Governance' and 'Digital India'.
Suggest a framework for the responsible and inclusive deployment of AI technologies in public administration.
GS3.imageDescription: A visual representation of AI interacting with government data and citizens, symbolizing e-governance.
GS3.trivia: India is among the top countries globally in terms of AI skill penetration and talent availability.

prelimsSyllabus.topic: Artificial Intelligence and its applications; E-governance.

GS3.headline: Expert Panel Recommends Stricter Measures for Conservation in Ecologically Sensitive Western Ghats
GS3.explainer:
• An expert committee constituted by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change submitted its report recommending stricter conservation norms for the Western Ghats.
• The report emphasizes the need to declare certain areas as Ecologically Sensitive Zones (ESZs) with regulated developmental activities.
• It highlights the immense biodiversity of the Western Ghats, which is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a global biodiversity hotspot.
• The recommendations aim to balance ecological preservation with the livelihood needs of local communities.
• Previous reports by Gadgil and Kasturirangan committees on Western Ghats conservation are also referenced, indicating ongoing policy debates.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Bio-diversity and Environment.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats span six states: Gujarat, Maharashtra, Goa, Karnataka, Kerala, and Tamil Nadu.
It is one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
The Gadgil Committee (Western Ghats Ecology Expert Panel - WGEEP) and Kasturirangan Committee (High-Level Working Group - HLWG) are key reports on the Western Ghats.
ESZs are areas notified under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats and the threats it faces from developmental activities.
Compare and contrast the recommendations of the Gadgil and Kasturirangan committees on Western Ghats conservation.
Analyze the challenges in implementing conservation policies in ecologically sensitive areas, particularly concerning local communities' livelihoods.
Suggest a balanced approach to ensure sustainable development while preserving the unique biodiversity of the Western Ghats.
GS3.imageDescription: A lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with misty mountains and dense forest.
GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayan mountain range.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Environment and Ecology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Conservation of biodiversity; Ecologically Sensitive Zones.

GS3.headline: India Strengthens Cyber Security Framework to Combat Rising Digital Threats
GS3.explainer:
• The National Cyber Security Coordinator announced new measures to bolster India's cyber security framework amidst a surge in sophisticated digital threats.
• The updated strategy focuses on enhancing critical information infrastructure protection, improving incident response mechanisms, and fostering public-private partnerships.
• CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) has issued fresh advisories, urging organizations to strengthen their cyber resilience against ransomware and data breaches.
• Emphasis is also placed on developing indigenous cyber security products and services, and skill development in the sector.
• This proactive approach aims to safeguard national security, economic stability, and citizen data in the digital realm.

prelimsPoints: CERT-In is the national agency for responding to computer security incidents.
Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) includes sectors like energy, transport, banking, and defense.

The Personal Data Protection Bill (now Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023) addresses data privacy.

GS3.headline: Government Reviews Agri-Export Policy to Boost Farmers' Income and Global Market Access
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry, in collaboration with the Ministry of Agriculture, initiated a review of India's Agri-Export Policy.
• The objective is to formulate strategies that boost agricultural exports, ensuring better price realization for farmers and higher income.
• Discussions focused on diversifying export baskets, addressing supply chain bottlenecks, and promoting value-added agricultural products.
• Measures include improving infrastructure for post-harvest management, cold storage facilities, and adherence to international quality standards.
• This move aligns with the government's vision of doubling farmers' income and enhancing India's presence in global agricultural markets.

GS3.headline: Government Undertakes Comprehensive Review of National Logistics Policy to Enhance Global Competitiveness
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry initiated a comprehensive review of the National Logistics Policy (NLP) launched in 2022.
• The review aims to identify bottlenecks and suggest measures to further reduce logistics costs and improve efficiency across supply chains.
• Focus areas include digitalization of logistics processes, development of multi-modal logistics parks, and skill development in the sector.
• The policy seeks to integrate various modes of transport and leverage technology to create a seamless logistics ecosystem.
• Enhanced logistics efficiency is crucial for boosting India's manufacturing competitiveness and achieving a $5 trillion economy goal.

GS3.headline: ICAR Develops New Climate-Resilient Crop Varieties to Combat Extreme Weather Events
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) announced the development of several new climate-resilient crop varieties.
• These varieties are engineered to withstand extreme weather conditions, including droughts, floods, and heatwaves, ensuring food security.
• The research focuses on enhancing crop productivity, nutritional value, and resistance to pests and diseases under changing climatic patterns.
• Farmers will receive training and support for adopting these new varieties, along with sustainable farming practices.
• This initiative is crucial for adapting Indian agriculture to climate change impacts and safeguarding farmers' livelihoods.

prelimsPoints: ICAR is an autonomous organization under the Department of Agricultural Research and Education (DARE), Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare.
India is highly vulnerable to climate change impacts on agriculture.
Climate-resilient crops can tolerate abiotic stresses like drought and salinity.
Food security is a major concern for India's large population.

GS3.headline: India's Second Indigenous Aircraft Carrier Commences Sea Trials, Boosting Naval Prowess
GS3.explainer:
• India's second indigenously built aircraft carrier, INS Vikrant, today commenced its final phase of sea trials, moving closer to full operational deployment.
• The carrier, built by Cochin Shipyard Limited, showcases India's growing capabilities in naval shipbuilding and self-reliance in defense production.
• Its operationalization will significantly enhance the Indian Navy's blue-water capabilities and projection of power in the Indian Ocean Region.
• The trials involve rigorous testing of the propulsion system, aviation complex, and various onboard systems.
• This milestone underscores India's commitment to becoming a net security provider and strengthening its maritime domain awareness.

Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.

It was built at Cochin Shipyard Limited.
India also operates INS Vikramaditya (modified Russian carrier).
Aircraft carriers are crucial for power projection and sea control.
